pomdp_formalization.gap_function: Delta(r) = R(r, sep) - R(r, mixed) = CER_mixed(r) - CER_sep(r)
pomdp_formalization.optimal_policy: threshold at r* where Delta(r*) = 0, Delta'(r*) > 0

bound3_aishell4_vacuity.note: On gold (g=0) Delta(r) has a single sign-change at r*~0.19, so the sharp-crossover curvature bound holds. Adding the silence dimension breaks the assumption in two ways as g grows: (i) at g=0.2 a SECOND sign-change appears (Delta goes + then - at high overlap, two crossovers), so there is no single r* to localize; (ii) at g>=0.4 the silence penalty dominates and Delta is negative everywhere (0 sign-changes, separated never wins), so the crossover vanishes entirely. At the AISHELL-4-like regime g=0.6 the gap is entirely negative (0 sign-changes). In both regimes the sharp-crossover assumption fails and Bounds 1 & 2 are vacuous: the O(1/n^2) bound has no single r* to anchor its constant.

lipschitz_constant.note: The silence-gap penalty is affine in g (RQ10 model), so the reward is exactly L-Lipschitz with L = max(|dR_sep/dg|, |dR_mixed/dg|) = max(HALLUCINATION_ADD=1.5, MILD_MASKING=0.1) = 1.5. Empirical estimate from the grid agrees.

H15a_regret_O(1/n)_on_gold.note: Derived the tighter O(1/n^2) curvature bound, which implies O(1/n). The log-log slope of the bound vs n is ~ -2.

H15b_bound_breaks_on_aishell4.note: On gold (g=0) Delta(r) has a single sign-change at r*~0.19, so the sharp-crossover curvature bound holds. Adding the silence dimension breaks the assumption in two ways as g grows: (i) at g=0.2 a SECOND sign-change appears (Delta goes + then - at high overlap, two crossovers), so there is no single r* to localize; (ii) at g>=0.4 the silence penalty dominates and Delta is negative everywhere (0 sign-changes, separated never wins), so the crossover vanishes entirely. At the AISHELL-4-like regime g=0.6 the gap is entirely negative (0 sign-changes). In both regimes the sharp-crossover assumption fails and Bounds 1 & 2 are vacuous: the O(1/n^2) bound has no single r* to anchor its constant.

H15c_lipschitz_restores_bound.note: With L-Lipschitz silence reward, the per-utterance POMDP on the (r, g) grid restores an O(L/n^2) bound.
